title: Red Hat Security Advisory: pam security update
overview:
An update for pam is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: pam security update

An update for pam is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for pam is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
pam-1.6.1-8.el10.src.rpm
pam-1.6.1-8.el10.x86_64.rpm
pam-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.x86_64.rpm
pam-debugsource-1.6.1-8.el10.x86_64.rpm
pam-devel-1.6.1-8.el10.x86_64.rpm
pam-libs-1.6.1-8.el10.x86_64.rpm
pam-libs-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.x86_64.rpm
pam-1.6.1-8.el10.s390x.rpm
pam-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.s390x.rpm
pam-debugsource-1.6.1-8.el10.s390x.rpm
pam-devel-1.6.1-8.el10.s390x.rpm
pam-libs-1.6.1-8.el10.s390x.rpm
pam-libs-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.s390x.rpm
pam-1.6.1-8.el10.ppc64le.rpm
pam-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.ppc64le.rpm
pam-debugsource-1.6.1-8.el10.ppc64le.rpm
pam-devel-1.6.1-8.el10.ppc64le.rpm
pam-libs-1.6.1-8.el10.ppc64le.rpm
pam-libs-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.ppc64le.rpm
pam-1.6.1-8.el10.aarch64.rpm
pam-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.aarch64.rpm
pam-debugsource-1.6.1-8.el10.aarch64.rpm
pam-devel-1.6.1-8.el10.aarch64.rpm
pam-libs-1.6.1-8.el10.aarch64.rpm
pam-libs-debuginfo-1.6.1-8.el10.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-6020: A flaw was found in linux-pam. The module pam_namespace may use access user-controlled paths without proper protection, allowing local users to elevate their privileges to root via multiple symlink attacks and race conditions.
